title: Racosperma spathulifolium
taxonomy: Racosperma spathulifolium is classified as a species within the genus Racosperma and the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Fabaceae. This plant is also known to be a part of the subgenus Acacia. However, it has been reclassified under the genus Acacia due to phylogenetic studies. The plant is native to Australia and is considered an Acacia species under the current classification system.
uses: Racosperma spathulifolium, also known as a species within the Acacia genus, has been traditionally used by indigenous Australian communities. The plant's leaves and pods have been applied to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
